scam_patterns.title: CBI Digital Arrest Scam
scam_patterns.description: Fraudster impersonates CBI/ED/Customs officer and claims victim is involved in money laundering or drug trafficking
example_messages: I am CBI officer. Your Aadhaar card is linked to money laundering. You will be arrested in 2 hours.
This is ED office. We have issued arrest warrant against you. Call immediately to avoid arrest.
Your parcel contains drugs. Customs department has filed FIR. Pay fine immediately to avoid arrest.
red_flags: Government officer contacting via WhatsApp/Telegram
Asking to stay on video call for hours
Demanding immediate payment to avoid arrest
Asking to not tell family members
Threatening immediate arrest
scam_patterns.legal_reference: IPC Section 419 (Cheating by personation) + IPC Section 420 (Cheating)
scam_patterns.recommended_action: Hang up immediately. Real CBI/ED never contacts via WhatsApp. Report to cybercrime.gov.in or call 1930.

scam_patterns.title: KYC Expiry Bank Scam
scam_patterns.description: Fraudster impersonates bank employee claiming KYC has expired and account will be blocked
example_messages: Your SBI KYC has expired. Your account will be blocked in 24 hours. Click link to update.
Dear customer, your HDFC bank KYC is pending. Send OTP to avoid account suspension.
Your bank account will be blocked. Update KYC immediately by sharing Aadhaar and PAN.
red_flags: Asking for OTP over phone or message
Sending suspicious links for KYC update
Asking for Aadhaar and PAN number via message
Creating urgency with account blocking threat
Poor grammar and spelling in message
scam_patterns.legal_reference: IPC Section 420 (Cheating) + IT Act Section 66C (Identity theft)
scam_patterns.recommended_action: Never share OTP. Visit bank branch directly for KYC. Report to cybercrime.gov.in.

scam_patterns.title: Lottery/Prize Scam
scam_patterns.description: Fraudster claims victim has won lottery or prize and asks for processing fee
example_messages: Congratulations! You have won Rs 25 lakh in KBC lottery. Pay processing fee of Rs 5000 to claim prize.
You are selected as lucky winner. Your prize is Rs 10 lakh. Send Rs 2000 registration fee.
Your mobile number won Rs 50 lakh in government lottery. Contact agent to claim prize.
red_flags: Asking for upfront fee to claim prize
You never entered any lottery
Asking for bank details to transfer prize
Pressure to act quickly before prize expires
Too good to be true amount
scam_patterns.legal_reference: IPC Section 420 (Cheating) + Prize Chits and Money Circulation Schemes Act
scam_patterns.recommended_action: Ignore and block. Legitimate lotteries never ask for upfront fees. Report to cybercrime.gov.in.

scam_patterns.title: UPI Payment Fraud
scam_patterns.description: Fraudster sends fake UPI collect requests or tricks victim into paying instead of receiving money
example_messages: I am buying your OLX item. I sent payment request on GPay. Please enter PIN to receive money.
Scan this QR code to receive your refund of Rs 5000 from Amazon.
Enter your UPI PIN to accept the payment I sent you.
red_flags: Asking to enter UPI PIN to RECEIVE money (you never enter PIN to receive)
Sending collect requests instead of paying directly
QR code that asks for payment instead of showing payment
Buyer asking you to scan QR to receive OLX payment
Fake screenshots of payment made
scam_patterns.legal_reference: IPC Section 420 (Cheating) + IT Act Section 66D (Cheating by impersonation using computer)
scam_patterns.recommended_action: You NEVER enter PIN to receive money. Reject all collect requests from unknown people. Report to cybercrime.gov.in.

scam_patterns.title: Fake Investment/Trading Scam
scam_patterns.description: Fraudster lures victim into fake investment platform promising high returns
example_messages: Join our WhatsApp group for guaranteed 30% monthly returns on stock market investment.
Our AI trading platform gives 500% returns. Invest Rs 10000 and get Rs 50000 in 30 days.
SEBI registered advisor here. I made Rs 5 lakh profit last month. Join my Telegram for free tips.
red_flags: Guaranteed high returns (no investment guarantees returns)
Asking to install unknown trading app
WhatsApp/Telegram groups with fake profit screenshots
Pressure to invest more after initial small profit
Difficulty withdrawing money later
scam_patterns.legal_reference: IPC Section 420 (Cheating) + SEBI Act violations + Banning of Unregulated Deposit Schemes Act
scam_patterns.recommended_action: Verify SEBI registration at sebi.gov.in. Never invest based on WhatsApp tips. Report to cybercrime.gov.in.

scam_patterns.title: Romance/Honey Trap Scam
scam_patterns.description: Fraudster builds fake romantic relationship online then asks for money
example_messages: I am US Army officer stationed in Syria. I want to send you gift but need customs fee of Rs 20000.
My dear, I am stuck at airport. My wallet stolen. Please send Rs 15000 I will return when I land.
I have feelings for you. I want to meet but need visa fees. Please help me Rs 50000.
red_flags: Met only online, never in person
Claims to be foreign national or army officer
Asks for money after building emotional connection
Always has emergency requiring money
Refuses video call or always has excuse
scam_patterns.legal_reference: IPC Section 420 (Cheating) + IPC Section 66A IT Act
scam_patterns.recommended_action: Never send money to someone you haven't met in person. Reverse image search their photo. Report to cybercrime.gov.in.

scam_patterns.title: Fake Courier/Parcel Scam
scam_patterns.description: Fraudster calls claiming illegal items found in victim's parcel at customs
example_messages: Your FedEx parcel contains fake passport and drugs. Customs officer will arrest you. Call immediately.
A parcel in your name has illegal items. Pay Rs 50000 to avoid police case.
DHL courier has been stopped. Your package has narcotics. Settle case in Rs 30000.
red_flags: Customs never calls about illegal items — they arrest directly
Asking for money to settle case over phone
Creating extreme fear and urgency
Asking to keep conversation secret from family
Multiple officers joining call to seem legitimate
scam_patterns.legal_reference: IPC Section 419 + IPC Section 420 + NDPS Act impersonation
scam_patterns.recommended_action: Real customs never asks for money over phone. Hang up immediately. Call 1930 to report.

scam_patterns.title: Fake Tech Support Scam
scam_patterns.description: Fraudster impersonates Microsoft/Google/telecom support claiming device is hacked
example_messages: Your Windows license has expired. Call Microsoft support immediately to avoid data loss.
Google detected suspicious activity on your account. Install AnyDesk for our engineer to fix.
Your Jio SIM shows illegal activity. Share OTP to verify identity and avoid disconnection.
red_flags: Microsoft/Google never calls you unsolicited
Asking to install remote access software (AnyDesk, TeamViewer)
Once they have remote access they can steal everything
Asking for payment for fake software license
Creating urgency about immediate data loss
scam_patterns.legal_reference: IPC Section 420 + IT Act Section 66 (Computer related offences)
scam_patterns.recommended_action: Never install remote access software for unknown callers. Real tech companies never call unsolicited. Report to cybercrime.gov.in.
